rulings.Case Title: STONERIDGE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. DEANZA PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complainant'S First Amended Cross-Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301 Per Ccp 170.6 Challenge To Judge Peter Busch)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2007, LINE 1 CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF CROSS-COMPLAINANTS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT 301 PER 170.6 CHALLENGE TO JUDGE BUSCH) IS GRANTED STRIKING ALLEGATIONS NOT RELATED TO FUTILITY. =(302/PJM/JF)

rulings.Case Title: STONERIDGE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. DEANZA PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To Answer To Cross-Complaint(Transferred From Dept. 301 Per Ccp 170.6 Challenge To Judge P. Busch)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2007, LINE 1 CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT(TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT 301 PER 170.6 CHALLENGE TO JUDGE BUSCH) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PREVIOUSLY, THE COURT GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO STATE FACTS WAS FUTILE TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DO SO. FURTHER IF PLAINTIFF WAS NOT REQUIRED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES OR ALLEGED FUTILITY, THE CITY IS IMMUNE FROM LIABILITY BECAUSE APPROVAL OF THE BUILDING PERMIT WAS A DISCRETIONARY ACT UNDER GOVT. CODE 815.6. CANCUN HOA V. CITY OF SAN JUAN CAPISTRANO(1989) 215 CAL.APP.3RD 1352. =(302/PJM/JF)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., INDIVIDUALLY AND AS EXECUT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2007, LINE 2 DEFENDANT ESTATE OF SHERWOOD J. ALLEN, DECEASED, KEYNOTE PROPERTIES, ASTRID LACITIS, ROBERT CAMOZZI, MARY WILLIAMS' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross Complaint IS DENIED.THE ALLEN PARTIES HAD A DUTY TO TO DETERMINE THE FACTS RELATED TO THE DAMAGE TO THEIR PROPERTY AND THERE ARE NO FACTS JUSTIFYING THE DELAY IN SEEKING TO FILE THIS CLAIM AGAINST SUNSET. THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT COULD NOT RELATE BACK FOR IT INVOLVES INTENTIONAL CONDUCT FOR DAMAGE TO PROPERTY THAT IS NOT THE SUBJECT OF THE STATE FARM CASE. THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT DO NOT STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES AND FALSE ADVERTISING. =(302/PJM/X-JM)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., INDIVIDUALLY AND AS EXECUT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Of Settlement [Ccp 877.6]
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2007, LINE 2 CROSS DEFENDANT SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Of Settlement [Ccp 877.6] IS GRANTED. THE SETTLEMENT MEETS THE REQUIREMENTS OF TECH-BILT. THERE ARE MATERIAL FACTUAL DISPUTES RELATED TO THE LIABILITY OF SUNSET AS WELL AS THE OWNERS AND PROPERTY MANAGERS GIVEN THE UNCERTAINTIES RELATED TO LIABILITY, THE SETTLEMENT IS A ROUGH APPROXIMATION OF PLAINTIFF'S POTENTIAL RECOVERY AND SUNSET'S PROPORTIONATE SHARE, RECOGNIZING THAT SUNSET SHOULD PAY LESS IN SETTLEMENT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED BASED ON REPRESENTATION OF STATE FARM AND SUNSET THAT THE WORK OF THE JOINT EXPERT, JAMES MCMULLEN, WILL NOT BE DISCLOSED, AND THAT ANY JOINT WORK PRODUCT MATERIALS POSSESSED BY SUNSET SHALL NOT BE DISCLOSED. ALLEN'S OBJECTIONS TO THE EVIDENCE IS OVERRULED. TO EVALUATE TECH BILT, IT IS NECESSARY FOR THE COURT TO EVALUATE THE FACTS THAT SUPPORT THE PARTIES STATE OF MIND. THE COURT IS NOT DETERMING LIABILITY AND THE FACTS AND ARGUMENTS OF COUNSEL ONLY GO TO ASSESSING THE GOOD FAITH OF THE SETTLEMENT. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE DOES NOT COMPLY WITH RULES OF COURT 3.113(m) AND 3.1306(c) AND FOR TAHT REASON THE REQUEST IS DENIED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL WORLD WATCH,INC.,IN THE PUBLIC VS. AMERICAN AIRLINES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Plaintiff Environmental World Watch, Inc.'S Motion For An Order 1. Substituting Anthony G. Graham, Esq. As Counsel For Plaintiff In Place Of Rueben Yeroushalmi, Esq. 2. Direct To Transfer File To New Counsel 3. Directing Rueben Yeroushalmi To Cease Any And All Communications With Defense Counsel 4. For Sanction In The Amount Of $9,460.00
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2007, LINE 3 HEARING ON PLAINTIFF ENVRONMENTAL WORLD WATCH, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER 1.SUBSTITUTING ANTHONY G. GRAHAM ESQ. AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IN PLACE OF RUEBEN YEROUSHALMI, ESQ. 2.DIRECT TO TRANSFER FILE TO NEW COUNSEL. 3.DIRECTING RUEBEN YEROUSHALMI TO CEASE ANY AND ALL COMMUNICATIONS WITH DEFENSE COUNSEL. 4.FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $9,460.00. THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. YEROUSHALMI IS ORDERED TO TURN OVER ALL CLIENT FILES IMMEDIATLEY. THE MOTION IS DENIED TO THE EXTENT IT SEEKS TO ORDER YEROUSHALMI TO CEASE COMMUNICATIONS, ON BEHALF OF OTHER CLIENTS, WITH DEFENDANT. THE MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED ONLY BECAUSE THE COURT IS NOT AWARE OF THE AUTHORITY TO AWARD SANCTIONS. BASED ON THIS RECORD, SANCTIONS COULD BE APPROPRIATE IF AUTHORIZED BY STATUTE OR CASE LAW. THE MOTION IS MOOT RELATED TO SUBSTITUTION OF COUNSEL FOR IT APPEARS THAT HAS OCCURRED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL WORLD WATCH, INC., IN THE PUBLIC VS. AIR JAMAICA LTD. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Plaintiff Environmental World Watch, Inc.'S Motion For An Order 1. Striking The "Notice Of Lien" Filed By Former Counsel Rubeben Yeroushalmi; And 2. Awarding Sanctions Of $6,545.00
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2007, LINE 5 HEARING ON PLAINTIFF ENVRONMENTAL WORLD WATCH, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER 1.STRIKING THE "NOTICE OF LIEN" FILED BY FORMER COUNSEL RUEBEN YEROUSHALMI. 2.AWARDING SANCTIONS OF $6,545.00. DENIED FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION. THE VALIDITY OF ANY REQUEST FOR FEES CAN BE EVALUATED SHOULD COUNSEL FILE AN APPLICATION FOR FEES AND/OR UPON APPROVAL OF ANY SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAULA SAVAGE VS. JONATHAN KISSEE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 12, 2007, LINE 8 PLAINTIFF PAULA SAVAGE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. IT IS PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE IN THAT IT SEEKS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BUT THE PAPERS ARE DIRECTED ONLY B&P 7031 CAUSE OF ACTION. THAT REQUIRES A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. SEE GONZALAS V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 189 CAL.APP.3RD 1542. PLAINTIFF'S ORIGIANL SEPARATE STATEMENT FAILS TO DISCLOSE THE AMOUNT PAID. ABSENT THE CONSENT OF DFENDANT, THE SUPPLEMENTAL STATEMENT CANNOT CURE THIS DEFECT. =(302/PJM/PB)
